name: Cave Entrance
description: A yawning cave mouth gapes before you, exhaling a breath of cold, stale air. The darkness inside is the kind that makes you question your life choices.

responses.look: The cave entrance looms like an open maw. Stalactites hang from the ceiling like nature's worst dental work. A faint dripping echoes from within.
responses.take: You consider taking the cave. Where would you even put it? Your pockets aren't that big.
responses.use: You're not entirely sure how one 'uses' a cave. Standing inside it, perhaps? That's just walking with extra steps.
responses.talk: You call into the darkness. 'Hello?' Your echo replies, sounding just as uncertain as you.
responses.open: The cave is already open. Has been for millennia. You're not that special.
responses.push: You push against the cave wall. The mountain does not yield. Shocking.
responses.pull: You try to pull the cave entrance wider. Geology disagrees with your approach.
responses.default: You contemplate the cave mouth. It contemplates you right back.

responses.look: A heavy oak door bound in iron. A rusted keyhole suggests someone once cared about privacy.
responses.use: It's locked. You'd need a key. Preferably one that isn't currently disintegrating.
responses.open: It's locked. The door rattles in its frame but holds firm. Somewhere, a locksmith is smirking.
responses.push: You lean into the door with all your weight. The door laughs silently at your efforts.
responses.pull: You pull on the door handle. Locked. From the other side. Of course.
responses.take: You can't take an entire door. Well, you could, but you'd need a much bigger inventory.
responses.talk: You knock politely. Nobody answers. Rude.
responses.default: You examine the locked door. It remains steadfastly locked.

responses.look: A small glass bottle perched on a rock ledge. The liquid inside shifts between colors you're fairly sure shouldn't exist in nature.
responses.use: Drink it? Here? Without even reading the label? Well, the label decomposed centuries ago, so that's a moot point.

responses.look: A mushroom growing on the cave wall, pulsing with an eerie blue bioluminescence. It's either magical or radioactive. Possibly both.

actions.text: You pluck the glowing mushroom from the wall. It pulses warmly in your hand, like a nightlight for the terminally adventurous.
You pocket the mysterious bottle. The liquid sloshes ominously. This is definitely the kind of decision that looks great in hindsight. Or terrible. One of those.
The rusty key grinds into the lock with a sound that makes your teeth ache. With a reluctant clunk, the door swings open. The key crumbles to rust dust in your hand. One-time use, apparently.
You wedge the glowing mushroom onto the stick. It holds, mostly. You've created a makeshift torch that casts an eerie blue glow. MacGyver would be... not exactly proud, but perhaps mildly amused.
You wedge the glowing mushroom onto the stick. It holds, mostly. You've created a makeshift torch that casts an eerie blue glow. MacGyver would be... not exactly proud, but perhaps mildly amused.

poison-death.title: Death by Curiosity
poison-death.narratorText: You drink the mysterious liquid. It tastes like regret and bad decisions. Which, coincidentally, is exactly what it is. You crumple to the floor in a heap of poor judgment.

dynamicDescriptions.door-unlocked: A yawning cave mouth gapes before you. The heavy oak door now hangs open, revealing deeper passages beyond. The cold air carries a faint whisper of adventure -- or possibly just a draft.
